entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Re: SAP IA MAG Stop producing empty elements
entry.content: Guess would be a combination of the existence of one source element - the ALCKZ value and the use of xsl:if. Would be worth trying xsl:choose instead and potentially adding an </xsl:otherwise> branch for no value.

entry.title: Re: E-Invoicing Germany - Transmission mode for ZUGFERD v...
entry.content:
Your understanding is correct: XRechnung is generally transmitted via the Peppol network or SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), while ZUGFeRD can be generated as a PDF with embedded XML and sent directly to customers via email without mandatory use of Peppol or DRC.

entry.title: Re: Approval workflow for Vendor, Customer and Material M...
entry.content:
Follow the below Best Practices

  1. Use APIs for upload rather than direct SSCUI changes, to allow workflow integration.
  2. Stage data in SAP BTP (via CAP or Build Process Automation) before posting to S/4HANA.
  3. Design Maker–Checker workflow in SAP Build Process Automation:
  4. Maker uploads file → workflow triggers approval task → Checker approves → API call posts data.
  5. Audit Trail: Ensure workflow logs are retained for compliance.
  6. Start with a pilot for one master data type (e.g., Business Partner) before extending to Materials.

So your next steps would be

  • Engage your SAP BTP team to design the Maker–Checker workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.
  • Use standard APIs for Customer, Vendor, and Material Master to integrate approved data.
  • Confirm with SAP whether future roadmap includes native workflow for master data in Public Cloud (currently not available).

entry.title: Re: FULL OUTER JOIN in ABAP SQL?
entry.content:
Hi,

yes this is what i would have suggested as well,

Just with left and right outer in the union branches , see below.

Depending on the database (or engines) used the union branches might be executed in parallel.
In my case the select returns 323 records (EKBE 3211 records  EKKN 582 records)  in 6.5 ms. The
UNION branches are executed in parallel.

With larger volumes (EKBE 20,001,016 rows and 117,896,463) it returns 8345 records in 160 ms.

Kind regards,

Hermann

SELECT COALESCE( ekkn~ebeln, ekbe~ebeln ) AS ebeln,
           COALESCE( ekkn~ebelp, ekbe~ebelp ) AS ebelp,
           COALESCE( ekkn~zekkn, ekbe~zekkn ) AS zekkn,
           ekbe~bewtp,
           ekkn~loekz
      FROM ekbe
           LEFT OUTER JOIN ekkn     " <=== FULL not valid in ABAP SQL
             ON  ekkn~ebeln = ekbe~ebeln
             AND ekkn~ebelp = ekbe~ebelp
             AND ekkn~zekkn = ekbe~zekkn
      WHERE ekbe~gjahr = '2026'
 UNION
  SELECT COALESCE( ekkn~ebeln, ekbe~ebeln ) AS ebeln,
           COALESCE( ekkn~ebelp, ekbe~ebelp ) AS ebelp,
           COALESCE( ekkn~zekkn, ekbe~zekkn ) AS zekkn,
           ekbe~bewtp,
           ekkn~loekz
      FROM ekbe
           RIGHT OUTER JOIN ekkn     " <=== FULL not valid in ABAP SQL
             ON  ekkn~ebeln = ekbe~ebeln
             AND ekkn~ebelp = ekbe~ebelp
             AND ekkn~zekkn = ekbe~zekkn
      WHERE ekkn~aedat = '20260101'
      INTO TABLE @DATA(itab).


SELECT COALESCE( ekkn~ebeln, ekbe~ebeln ) AS ebeln,
           COALESCE( ekkn~ebelp, ekbe~ebelp ) AS ebelp,
           COALESCE( ekkn~zekkn, ekbe~zekkn ) AS zekkn,
           ekbe~bewtp,
           ekkn~loekz
      FROM ekbe
           LEFT OUTER JOIN ekkn     " <=== FULL not valid in ABAP SQL
             ON  ekkn~ebeln = ekbe~ebeln
             AND ekkn~ebelp = ekbe~ebelp
             AND ekkn~zekkn = ekbe~zekkn
      WHERE ekbe~gjahr = '2026'
 UNION
  SELECT COALESCE( ekkn~ebeln, ekbe~ebeln ) AS ebeln,
           COALESCE( ekkn~ebelp, ekbe~ebelp ) AS ebelp,
           COALESCE( ekkn~zekkn, ekbe~zekkn ) AS zekkn,
           ekbe~bewtp,
           ekkn~loekz
      FROM ekbe
           RIGHT OUTER JOIN ekkn     " <=== FULL not valid in ABAP SQL
             ON  ekkn~ebeln = ekbe~ebeln
             AND ekkn~ebelp = ekbe~ebelp
             AND ekkn~zekkn = ekbe~zekkn
      WHERE ekkn~aedat = '20260101'
      INTO TABLE @DATA(itab).

entry.title: How to Establish Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with SAP
entry.content:
Why Carbon Footprint Management Matters

Measuring emissions is essential for any sustainability strategy. Without accurate data, organizations cannot effectively reduce their environmental impact or meet regulatory requirements.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables companies to calculate carbon emissions across their operations, from individual activities to the entire corporate footprint.

With this solution, businesses can:

  • Calculate emissions across GHG Scope 1, 2, and 3
  • Gain transparency into their environmental impact
  • Support regulatory reporting and compliance
  • Build a foundation for decarbonization initiatives

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This two-hour course provides a comprehensive introduction to corporate carbon footprint management:

GHG Protocol and Regulations

Understand the fundamentals of the Greenhouse Gas Protocol and key regulatory frameworks.

Introduction to SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Explore the features, capabilities, and architecture of the solution and how it supports carbon tracking across products, operations, and value chains.

System Setup and Configuration

Learn how to set up SAP Sustainability Footprint Management in your SAP Business Technology Platform environment.

Managing Emissions Data

Maintain source data and prepare it for accurate emissions calculation.

Calculating Carbon Footprints

Calculate emissions across Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 and analyze the results.

Integrating with Corporate Steering

Share calculated emissions data with SAP Sustainability Control Tower to support reporting and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with sustainability and reporting
  • Consultants implementing carbon management solutions
  • Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

No prerequisites are required, making it suitable for beginners.

Start building a solid foundation for carbon management and explore the Learning Journey “Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration” here.

entry.title: Re: Shipment Classification accessible via menu path from...
entry.content: Your observation is correct.  The menu path for classification is standard for Customer/Vendor/Material masters but not available for Shipment documents. To achieve the same behavior for shipments (like Z90), you would need a custom enhancement; it cannot be activated by configuration alone.

entry.title: Re: unable to delete PR in service order
entry.content:
Hi,

Set the Deletion Indicator on the PO line item in ME22N first, then clear any open GR/IR balance using MR11, and finally set the deletion indicator on the PR via ME52N or deleting operations in maintenance order the PR cannot be deleted as long as an active PO reference exists against it.

Regards

entry.title: Re: QM Notification - "An inspection lot on sample plant...
entry.content:
Hi,

Check for an existing open inspection lot in QA33 for the same Material + Plant + Inspection Type, and post the Usage Decision (UD) in QA11 to close it. further share screenshot of error.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: unable to delete PR in service order
entry.content:
In SAP ECC (assuming you are in ECC as you have not mentioned about this), a Purchase Requisition (PR) cannot always be deleted once it has been consumed in downstream documents, even if you reverse the Goods Receipt and delete the relevant order operation. That is why you are seeing the “Deletion flag” option greyed out for some PRs, while in other cases it works.

Having said that, the Best Practice is instead of deleting, mark the PR as Closed or Rejected.

Also, below is a comparison table

entry.title: Re: Annual inventory report/compensation for similar prod...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA, the Physical Inventory / Annual Inventory process via MI01/MI04/MI07 does support difference posting, however netting or compensation between different storage locations or materials is not allowed by standard SAP each material/storage location difference must be posted individually as a surplus or deficit.

Regards

entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 update continues our commitment to delivering intelligent, flexible, and user-centric enhancements. This update introduces powerful new capabilities specifically designed to streamline processes for Solution Order Specialists. New capabilities have been introduced covering the full scope of solution selling with SAP Cloud ERP, and in this blog, we’ll look at them in more detail.

Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy related Pricing in Solution Orders

 

You can now leverage product hierarchies directly within the Manage Solution Orders app. Product hierarchies are a cornerstone among the generic features for organizing products, or materials, across multiple levels. This structured approach is particularly valuable for complex pricing scenarios. When your price calculations are configured to depend on product hierarchy data, you can now use these hierarchies in your solution orders for precise and automated price determination, simplifying a critical part of the sales process.

Similar to the enhancement for products, you can now utilize customer hierarchies in the Manage Solution Orders app. Organizing customers into multi-level hierarchies is often essential for managing pricing agreements and sales strategies. With this feature, you can now use customer hierarchies within solution orders for price determination, provided your pricing procedures are set up to recognize this data. This enables more accurate, automated pricing tailored to your customer structures.

For an in-depth look at customer and product hierarchies in solution order management, take a look at this blog

Sales Item Enhancements in 2602

Enhanced Flexibility with New Item Categories and Process Variants

As a solution order specialist, compiling sales orders that meet specific business needs and align with customer agreements is essential. This requires a high degree of flexibility, With 2602, we are extending solution orders to cover sales processes that were previously unsupported for configurable products and sales kits. The Manage Solution Orders app is made more versatile with the introduction of new item categories and process variants, opening the door for selling high-value, make-to-order items.

 



From 2602, we support milestone billing in solution orders. Milestone billing is handled with a billing plan that splits total sales-order value across predefined billing dates, instead of billing everything at once -typically used for long duration deliveries or projects. Introducing this capability also applies milestone-based billing to established sales kit scenarios such as ERLA. LUMF and CPFH. Furthermore, it provides solution order specialists with the option to manage down payments as part of solution selling.

 

Alongside milestone-based billing, we introduce the ability to run advanced down payment (ADP) processing for sales orders that are created as follow-ups to solution orders. This enhancement integrates your solution order process more tightly with your financial operations, securing revenue upfront for large or custom orders. Before utilizing this feature, a configuration expert must ensure the condition types DDPR and DDPS have been added to the pricing procedure used for your solution orders.

New item categories introduced to the manage solution orders app support third-party sales with variant configuration. We have expanded solution order capabilities for configurable products by adding support for third-party shipment scenarios. This should help business users handle a broader range of sales scenarios within a single framework and better orchestrate complex fulfillment models involving external suppliers. This enhancement makes it possible to manage third-party sales in make-to-order processes, both with and without charge, giving companies more flexibility in how they structure customer-specific solution offerings.

 

Overall, these changes strengthen solution selling by enabling more adaptable end-to-end sales processes. They also improve business-user productivity by making it easier to manage customer-specific requirements through a more flexible and comprehensive solution order process.

This update is critical for businesses that manage high-value sales items, as it helps optimize cash flow through milestone-based invoicing. It also delivers a more flexible, customer-centric approach to sales order processing by better accommodating complex product configurations in third-party scenarios.

See the documentation for a full overview of new item categories

 

Set Billing Blocks for Release and Completed Sales Items (2602.1)

A helpful enhancement in the Manage Solution Orders app now lets users set or reset billing blocks even after sales items have reached Released or Completed status. That means billing issues can be corrected later in the process without reopening documents, helping teams work more efficiently and keep billing moving. Billing blocks are maintained by specifying the relevant billing block reason. Previously, this was only possible while sales items were still Open or In Process. There are a couple of important conditions to keep in mind: this feature is available for sales items released for follow-up sales orders of type OR, while items released for sales orders of type SOR have always allowed billing block updates. The same capability is also available through the business solution order APIs, including API_BUS_SOLUTION_ORDER_SRV and BusinessSolutionOrder.

Cumulated net value field for solution order items in the solution order output form

 

Solution bundles let you sell a combined offering in a solution order using one bundle item with predefined subitems such as products, services, projects, contracts, or subscriptions. SAP treats the bundle item as a configurable product and, after release, generates the relevant follow-up documents for the underlying subitems. When selling preconfigured bundles, customers wish to indicate the bundle price in the order confirmation they issue to customers to provide more transparency. As a result, with 2602, we make a cumulated net value field available in the output interface and forms. This shows the aggregated price of the purchased bundle without a breakdown of the individual items in the bundle.

 

Transfer of Transactional Address to Sales Orders

To provide greater flexibility in managing address information for complex business scenarios, business users can now define specific transactional addresses for business partners (category as organization) at the solution order level. This address information is then seamlessly transferred to any subsequent sales orders created from that solution order.

Note: While a change of address on subsequent documents is permitted and will be reflected throughout the document chain, this action is recommended only for making minor typographical corrections to maintain data consistency.


Filter by Solution Order When Scheduling Billing Document Creation (2508.1)

 

A feature which was released in the first micro-delivery of the 2508 release which we want to draw your attention to is the support of scheduled billing creation with solution orders. Previously, filtering by solution order was only available in the Create Billing Documents app. Now, the same capability is available in Schedule Billing Creation, allowing users either to select billing-relevant documents for specific solution orders or to include all solution-order-related billing due list items in a recurring job. This supports both specialized solution-selling scenarios, where only selected solution orders should be billed, and broader operational setups where solution-order processing is used more widely alongside other sales scenarios.

To support this, we have introduced a new Solution Order field under SD Document Data in the app. As part of the UI update, the SD Document field was also moved under SD Document Data, and Documents to Be Selected was renamed to SD Documents to Be Selected. Overall, this enhancement helps organizations automate invoicing for solution-order-based business more efficiently, reduce manual effort, and process larger billing volumes faster.

 

Filtered Date Rules for Service Contract Billing in Solution Orders

We have increased clarity and convenience in billing plan management. You can now use filtered date rules, configured by key users, for the billing plan of service contract items in solution orders. This applies to both settlement period rules and billing date rules. Previously, the Manage Solution Orders app displayed all available rules, which required users to rely on descriptive texts to find the correct one. This new, filtered approach ensures that only relevant rules are presented, significantly reducing errors and improving user efficiency.

Subscription Items Enhancements in Solution Order Management (2508.3)

Selling subscriptions within solution orders has been significantly enhanced in the 2508 microdelivery releases, and further with 2602. Below, you’ll find an overview of key new capabilities introduced to make subscription selling more reliable, flexible, and user-friendly.


Subscription Validation in Solution Orders (2508.3)

One of the most valuable updates is support for the subscription validation extension point from SAP Subscription Billing. This helps business users prevent the creation of incorrect or incomplete follow-up subscriptions by validating subscription data during simulation and release. As a result, errors can be caught earlier in the process, improving productivity and reducing the need for later corrections.

 


Auto-Locking for subscription item prices (2508.3)

A major usability improvement is the new Auto-Lock pricing capability. With this feature, prices can be locked automatically once a subscription is created, protecting the agreed subscription price from later changes in the underlying product list price. In the Solution Order UI, each price element now shows a Customization Status, indicating whether it is customizable or non-customizable, along with a new Auto-Lock column that shows whether the price is currently locked. This gives users much better transparency into pricing behavior and helps improve customer satisfaction by ensuring stable subscription pricing over time.

The enhancement supports three pricing scenarios: customizable only, auto-locked only, and both customizable and auto-locked. When both settings are enabled, the price can be customized once, after which the auto-lock is removed. These capabilities are powered by new fields in the subscription API, which provide the necessary status information and ensure a more consistent and intuitive user experience across the process.

The release also increases flexibility in commercial subscription models by adding support for both credit and charge rate elements. This means businesses can now model one-time credits as well as one-time charges directly in Solution Order, enabling more creative and customer-friendly offerings such as onboarding credits, promotional incentives, or service compensation.

Alternative currencies for subscription items

Subscriptions can now be created in an alternate market currency, making it easier to satisfy customer-specific regional or contractual requirements when the subscription currency differs from the main Solution Order transaction currency. Together, these enhancements make subscription handling more robust, transparent, and adaptable for real-world solution selling.

 

Integration

Simulation API for Solution Orders for external Quoting applications

Building on the 2508 release, we have made further enhancements to the Solution Order API with OData V4 simulation capabilities that allow businesses to simulate solution orders without creating actual transactional documents. This gives external quoting and sales applications access to SAP Cloud ERP logic in real time, making it easier to generate accurate quotes based on partner determination, pricing, and billing plan simulation.

The API returns detailed simulation results at both header and item level, including net value, partners, price elements, and contract billing plans for solution orders and their items. It supports a broad range of item types, such as sales items, service contract items, and service order items, while also covering scenarios like sales kits, service bundles, and preconfigured solution bundles.

These capabilities improve integration flexibility and support more seamless orchestration across external sales tools and SAP Cloud ERP. With built-in extensibility and secure OAuth 2.0-based integration, the Solution Order API provides a strong foundation for connected sales processes, while future roadmap plans include support for configurable products, parameterized solution bundles, and subscription products.

 

 


Extensibility Enhancements

Enable Subscription billing cloud BAdI for developer extensibility

Extensibility is a critical success factor in customer implementation projects, especially when standard capabilities in solution business and service management need to be enhanced to meet specific business requirements. Recent innovations strengthen both developer and side-by-side extensibility. On the developer extensibility side, READ access through Entity Manipulation Language (EML) is now available for BAdI CL_CRMS4_SERV_CHCK_BEFORE_SAVE, enabling the use of RAP façades to access the transient data buffer introduced in release 2602. In addition, BAdI CRMS4_SOLO_ORC_SB_SUBSC_MODIFY has now been officially released, giving developers more flexibility to adapt subscription-related processes. For side-by-side extensibility, “derived” events based on standard events make it possible to enrich event payloads and define context attributes for more precise filtering. This capability is already available for Service Contract in 2602, with Solution Order support planned from 2608 onward.



 

 Find all our assets

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

 

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you: 

 

 

 

 ️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Asset Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Re: Shipment Classification accessible via menu path from...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Classification shortcut in customer master (XD02) but forgot to do the same for Shipment transaction (VT02N), so users are forced to go to CL20N separately.

Your cleanest fix without modification is to create a small Z-transaction that accepts a shipment number and calls FM CLAF_CLASSIFICATION_OF_OBJECTS with class type Z90, then add it to user menus as a workaround.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Pressing the "Display JIT Calls" button in the VL03N...
entry.content: The button only works if the delivery is technically linked to a JIT call. So if you are confident that there is a linkage between the delivery and JIT call and still see nothing, it is likely a release-specific bug, and SAP Notes or OSS support will be needed
